title: Ina's Mac & Cheese
description: From Barefoot Contessa Family Style — Ina's legendary baked mac and cheese with a crispy breadcrumb topping. Three cheeses (Gruyère, sharp cheddar, and extra-sharp cheddar) make a sauce so rich and flavorful it'll ruin boxed mac and cheese for you forever. The centerpiece of her Pasta Night family dinner menu.

ingredients.item: Fresh white bread crumbs (5 slices, crusts removed)

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F. Drizzle oil into a large pot of boiling salted water. Add the macaroni and cook according to the directions on the package, 6 to 8 minutes. Drain well.

Meanwhile, heat the milk in a small saucepan, but don't boil it. Melt 6 tablespoons of butter in a large (4-quart) pot and add the flour. Cook over low heat for 2 minutes, stirring with a whisk. While whisking, add the hot milk and cook for a minute or two more, until thickened and smooth. Off the heat, add the Gruyère, cheddar, 1 tablespoon salt, the pepper, and nutmeg. Add the cooked macaroni and stir well.
Pour into a 3-quart baking dish. Combine the bread crumbs and the remaining ½ cup Gruyère and sprinkle on top. Dot with the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until the sauce is bubbly and the macaroni is browned on the top. Serve hot.
tips: Gruyère is the secret weapon here — it melts beautifully and has a nutty depth that cheddar alone can't match.
Fresh bread crumbs (made from white bread in a food processor) give a much better topping than dried.
Cavatappi (corkscrew pasta) holds the sauce better than regular elbows.
This can be assembled ahead and refrigerated, then baked when needed — add 10 minutes to the baking time.
Source: Barefoot Contessa Family Style by Ina Garten (2002)
